roadmap.amendment_note: Phase 03 re-sized after the S-DBN-CHURN RCA (docs/analysis/root-cause-analysis-in-flight-churn-fail-fast-gap.md) + the ADR-0070 A1 amendment (2026-07-01). The prior single step 03-01 ('un-ignore S-DBN-CHURN, test-only, no production src') was MIS-SIZED: a clean backend FIN is invisible to v1's (B) self-teardown + (C) TCP_USER_TIMEOUT, so the AT cannot go green without (a) the A1 production pump half-close-forward and (b) the T1/T2 test-model fix. Phase 03 now holds two steps: 03-01 (A1 production + test-model fix, mutation-gated) then 03-02 (the un-ignore, now a real un-ignore→GREEN gate). Steps 04-01 → 04-01 unchanged (terminal gate).

phases.name: Replace action — the walking skeleton (US-BIR-1, slice-01)

steps.name: TxnOp::IncrementU64 store primitive — atomic monotonic bump-and-clear
criteria: S-BIR-TXN-01: a #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/txn_increment_u64.rs (gated integration-tests, real LocalIntentStore over a tempfile::TempDir) proves that a single store.txn(vec![TxnOp::IncrementU64{gen_key}, TxnOp::Delete{stop_key}]) — given no workloads/payments/generation key and a present workloads/payments/stop sentinel — returns TxnOutcome::Committed AND store.get(gen_key) decodes (big-endian u64) to exactly 1 AND store.get(stop_key) returns None, asserting the atomicity contract that no observer sees the gen bumped without the stop cleared or vice versa.

S-BIR-TXN-02 (THE load-bearing concurrency proof, ADR-0073 § item 4): a #[tokio::test] in txn_increment_u64.rs spawns N concurrent tasks (e.g. N=16 or 32 via a JoinSet) each committing txn(vec![IncrementU64{gen_key}, Delete{stop_key}]) against the SAME LocalIntentStore, then proves every txn returned Committed AND the final store.get(gen_key) decodes BE-u64 to exactly N (no lost bump — redb serialises writers so each read-modify-write sees the prior committed value) AND the value never went backwards. This is the @property @concurrency proof, NOT a single example.

S-BIR-TXN-03: a #[tokio::test] in txn_increment_u64.rs proves the absent-key edge — given a LocalIntentStore with neither a generation key nor a stop sentinel, a txn(vec![IncrementU64{gen_key}, Delete{stop_key}]) commits, get(gen_key) decodes to 1 (absent read defaults to 0 then +1), and the Delete of the already-absent stop_key is a no-op (Committed, not an error — the running-origin restart deletes a /stop that was never written).

S-BIR-TXN-04: a #[tokio::test] in txn_increment_u64.rs proves the corrupt-row edge per development.md § 'Safe byte-slice access' — given a generation key holding a 3-byte (non-8) value, a txn(vec![IncrementU64{gen_key}]) commits, the BE-u64 read defends against the short slice (decodes to 0 via a length-checked <[u8;8]>::try_from(..).map(u64::from_be_bytes) with a 0 fallback, NEVER bytes[0..8] indexing, NEVER a panic), and get(gen_key) decodes to 1.

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design') — build EXACTLY the TxnOp::IncrementU64 surface ADR-0073 § 'The six pinned signatures' item 4 names; invent no surface beyond it. Land: (a) the TxnOp::IncrementU64 variant on crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/intent_store.rs, carrying the full rustdoc behavior contract per development.md § 'Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature' (the preconditions/postconditions/edge-cases/observable-invariant are spelled out in ADR-0073 item 4 — transcribe that contract onto the trait, do not paraphrase a weaker one); (b) the IncrementU64 read-modify-write match arm in crates/overdrive-store-local/src/redb_backend.rs following the shipped put_if_absent get-then-insert-inside-one-begin_write/commit shape (ADR-0073 item 4 pins the algorithm); (c) the exhaustive-match arms on the two test-double impls FaultInjectingIntentStore + CountingIntentStore. NO get-then-Put at any call site, NO Conflict retry — LocalIntentStore::txn returns Committed unconditionally and Conflict stays dead forward-looking RaftStore surface (ADR-0073 § 'On the dead TxnOutcome::Conflict variant').

RED convention (per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'RED scaffolds'): the IncrementU64 redb match arm + the two test-double arms land RED via todo!("RED scaffold: IncrementU64 atomic monotonic bump") with a file/module-scoped #[expect(clippy::todo, reason = "RED scaffold; lands GREEN in step 01-01")]; the txn_increment_u64.rs test scaffolds land RED via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] (use ( ) not { } in panic strings per feedback_panic_format_string_brace_escaping); the RED→GREEN→COMMIT cycle (ADR-025) flips them, fail-for-right-reason gated by distill/red-classification.md (every TXN scenario REDs MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY).

Mutation gate (MANDATORY store-primitive surface; the shared per-step invocation in validation.mutation_invocation; --package overdrive-store-local --file crates/overdrive-store-local/src/redb_backend.rs): kill-rate ≥ 80%. KILL TARGET: a mutation on the IncrementU64 arm swapping the inner +1 → +0 (or dropping the saturating add, or reading a stale snapshot instead of the live row) MUST be killed by S-BIR-TXN-02.
steps.scenario_name: S-BIR-TXN-01..04 — one atomic txn bumps the generation and clears the stop sentinel; concurrent bumps never lose an increment; absent and corrupt rows read as zero

steps.name: Desired-run generation precursor + current-instance-scoped reconciler veto
criteria: S-BIR-RESTART-STOPPED (R4): a #[test] in crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_lifecycle_restart.rs constructs (desired, actual, view, tick) for a declared workload 'payments' whose only alloc 'payments-0' is Terminated{by: Operator}, with desired.generation=1 and view.observed_generation=0 (restart_pending), invokes the pure reconcile(), and proves the returned Vec<Action> contains exactly one StartAllocation whose minted id ≠ payments-0 (mint_alloc_id(attempt=allocs_vec.len()) ⇒ payments-1, new /30) AND the returned NextView.observed_generation == 1 AND no Action withdraws intent. Asserts only on the returned (Vec<Action>, NextView) tuple, never a private View field.

S-BIR-RESTART-RUNNING-STOP (R2): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs for 'coinflip' whose 'coinflip-0' is Running, desired.generation=1, observed=0, proves reconcile() emits exactly one StopAllocation for coinflip-0 with terminal Stopped{by: Operator} AND NextView.observed_generation == 0 (UNCHANGED — the load-bearing ordering: the stamp must NOT happen on the stop tick, else observed==desired re-arms the veto before the fresh instance exists, stranding the workload Terminated).

S-BIR-RESTART-RUNNING-PLACE (R3): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs for a running-origin restart that already stopped coinflip-0 (now Terminated{by: Operator}, no Running alloc remains), desired.generation=1, observed=0, proves reconcile() emits StartAllocation for a fresh coinflip-1 (A1 ≠ A2, new /30) AND NextView.observed_generation == 1 (the placement tick is the only tick that stamps).

S-BIR-STOP-ONCE (R5): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs for a running-origin restart that emitted StopAllocation for coinflip-0 on tick 1, reconciling again while coinflip-0 is still draining (not yet Terminated), proves the tick-2 action set contains NO second StopAllocation for the draining alloc AND observed_generation is still unstamped — the no-duplicate-stop contract while the old instance drains.

S-BIR-COALESCE-PLACE (DDD-10, level-triggered): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs for a stopped-origin 'payments' with observed_generation=0 where two pre-placement restarts advanced desired_generation to 2, proves reconcile() places exactly ONE StartAllocation for the latest generation AND stamps NextView.observed_generation == 2 (= desired, NOT observed+1) — the stamp is observed=desired, which makes the machine coalesce by construction.

S-BIR-COALESCE-NO-REPLAY (DDD-10): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs given a coalesced placement already stamped observed_generation == desired_generation (=2) with payments-1 placed, proves a follow-up reconcile() finds restart_pending false (observed == desired) and emits NO further StartAllocation AND the generation never goes backwards (NextView.observed_generation == 2 unchanged). Together with S-BIR-COALESCE-PLACE this kills the observed+1 mutation (under observed+1 the placement would leave observed(1)<desired(2) → a second StartAllocation here).

S-BIR-SEQUENTIAL (DDD-10): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs given the prior restart placed payments-1 (Running) and stamped observed_generation=1, and a second restart has since advanced desired_generation to 2 (observed 1 < desired 2), proves reconcile() finds restart_pending true again and re-enters the cycle — emits StopAllocation for the current payments-1 AND does NOT stamp observed_generation on this tick (the fresh payments-2 has not been placed). Pins the sequential-vs-concurrent distinction (the second-restart-advanced-generation is Given context, one driving reconcile()).

S-BIR-REGRESSION-STOPPED (R1-crash, DDD-13 — MANDATORY mutation case): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs given a stopped-origin restart placed payments-1 which reached Running then CRASHED (terminal Failed / Terminated with a crash reason, NOT Stopped{Operator}), the superseded payments-0 Terminated{by: Operator} row retained, observed_generation == desired_generation (restart_pending false), proves reconcile() crash-restarts the fresh instance (emits RestartAllocation for payments-1 / a new Running converges) AND does NOT return an empty action set wedged on the stale payments-0/Operator row. current_alloc(&allocs_vec) is the crashed payments-1 (a crash reason, not Operator), so the scoped veto does NOT fire and the Run branch falls through to the existing is_restartable/backoff branch.

S-BIR-REGRESSION-NUMERIC (DDD-13, review HIGH-3 — the numeric-vs-lexical 'current instance' invariant proven AT THE DRIVING PORT, not only via the helper): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs given a workload whose retained alloc history has reached double digits — a CRASHED current payments-10 (a crash reason) alongside a lexically-later but numerically-earlier superseded payments-2 Terminated{by: Operator}, observed_generation == desired_generation (restart_pending false) — proves reconcile() crash-restarts payments-10 (it is the numeric-max current instance) and is NOT wedged by the lexically-later payments-2/Operator row. Pins the operator-visible behavior of the numeric current_alloc selection through reconcile() itself (complements the pure-fn proptest S-BIR-CURRENT-ALLOC): a mutation flipping current_alloc to lexical max would make payments-2/Operator the 'current' instance, fire the veto, and wedge the crashed payments-10 — killed here at the reconciler boundary, not only at the helper.

S-BIR-REGRESSION-RUNNING (R1-crash, DDD-13 — MANDATORY mutation case): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs given a running-origin restart cycled coinflip-0 → fresh coinflip-1 reached Running then CRASHED (a crash reason, NOT Stopped{Operator}), the now-superseded coinflip-0 Terminated{by: Operator} row retained, restart_pending false, proves reconcile() crash-restarts coinflip-1 and is NOT wedged on the superseded coinflip-0/Operator row. The two regression cases are the two halves the iteration-3 fix pins forever.

S-BIR-BUG3-PRESERVED (DDD-7, the other half of the scoped veto): a #[test] in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs given a declared 'payments' whose current alloc payments-0 is Terminated{by: Operator} and a same-spec deploy that did NOT bump the generation (observed == desired, restart_pending false), proves the current-instance-scoped veto fires (current_alloc is the operator-stopped payments-0) and reconcile() places NO StartAllocation (the workload stays stopped — a re-deploy does NOT resurrect an operator-stopped workload; scoping narrows which row arms the veto, it never weakens it).

S-BIR-CURRENT-ALLOC (DDD-13, @property): a proptest in workload_lifecycle_restart.rs given alloc rows payments-0 … payments-10 with mixed terminal/running states proves current_alloc(&rows) returns the row whose mint_alloc_id attempt suffix is numerically maximal (… payments-10) and NOT the lexical max (AllocationId is Ord on the raw string, so BTreeMap/.values() order is LEXICAL — alloc-payments-10 < alloc-payments-2 — the helper MUST parse the numeric suffix; the never-delete invariant makes attempt indices strictly increasing so the numeric max is unambiguously current). proptest is the paradigm.

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design') — build EXACTLY the reconciler edit ADR-0073 § 'The six pinned signatures' item 5 (+ the current_alloc helper) names; invent no surface beyond it. In crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/workload_lifecycle.rs: WorkloadLifecycleState.generation: u64 (hydrated from workloads/<id>/generation, absent ⇒ 0); WorkloadLifecycleView.observed_generation: u64 with #[serde(default)] (additive CBOR field per development.md § 'Reconciler I/O → Schema evolution', NO envelope bump); the pure helper current_alloc co-located with mint_alloc_id (numeric max of the parsed <N> suffix, a non-parseable suffix sorts below any parseable one — exact contract in ADR-0073 item 5); the scoped-veto edit (restart_pending = observed_generation < desired.generation; veto = !restart_pending && current_alloc(&allocs_vec).is_some_and(is_operator_stopped) — NOT allocs_vec.iter().any(is_operator_stopped)); stamp next_view.observed_generation = desired.generation on the placement tick (R3/R4), NEVER the stop tick (R2/R5); plus the hydrate_desired generation read in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs (sibling of stop_intent_present). NO generation field on the rkyv AllocStatusRow (no ADR-0048 envelope bump), NO View bookkeeping of consumed Operator-stop ids.

RED convention (per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'RED scaffolds'): the generation/observed_generation fields + the current_alloc body + the veto edit + the placement stamp land RED via todo!("RED scaffold: generation gate + current-instance-scoped veto") with #[expect(clippy::todo, reason = "RED scaffold; lands GREEN in step 01-02")] (the reconcile()-walking adjacent acceptance tests that touch the new match get the same #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] until the edit lands per § 'Downstream fallout'); workload_lifecycle_restart.rs test scaffolds land RED via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]; RED→GREEN→COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md (every scenario REDs MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY).

Mutation gate (MANDATORY reconciler-logic surface; the shared per-step invocation in validation.mutation_invocation; --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/workload_lifecycle.rs): kill-rate ≥ 80%, with the MANDATORY kill targets: a mutation reverting the veto to allocs_vec.iter().any(is_operator_stopped) (or dropping the current_alloc(...) scoping) MUST be killed by S-BIR-REGRESSION-STOPPED/-RUNNING; a mutation changing the stamp observed=desired → observed+1 MUST be killed by S-BIR-COALESCE-PLACE/-NO-REPLAY; a mutation flipping current_alloc to .values().last()/lexical-max MUST be killed by S-BIR-CURRENT-ALLOC AND S-BIR-REGRESSION-NUMERIC (the latter pins it at the reconciler boundary); a mutation that makes the scoped veto never fire MUST be killed by S-BIR-BUG3-PRESERVED; the observed_generation < desired comparison flipped to <=/== MUST be killed by S-BIR-SEQUENTIAL.
steps.scenario_name: S-BIR-RESTART-STOPPED/-RUNNING-STOP/-RUNNING-PLACE, S-BIR-STOP-ONCE, S-BIR-COALESCE-PLACE/-NO-REPLAY, S-BIR-SEQUENTIAL, S-BIR-REGRESSION-STOPPED/-RUNNING/-NUMERIC, S-BIR-BUG3-PRESERVED, S-BIR-CURRENT-ALLOC — generation-gated placement, level-triggered coalescing, the current-instance-scoped veto + crash-restart regression, numeric current-instance selection (proven both pure-fn and through reconcile())

steps.name: restart_workload HTTP handler + api types + POST /v1/jobs/:id/restart route
criteria: S-BIR-HANDLER-404 (US-BIR-1 AC5): a #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/restart_workload_unknown.rs (sibling of job_stop_unknown.rs) given no workloads/nonexistent aggregate proves restart_workload returns Err(ControlPlaneError::NotFound { resource: "workloads/nonexistent" }) AND no IntentStore txn is committed (zero txn calls via a counting / fault-injecting IntentStore double — no generation bump, no sentinel delete) AND no job-lifecycle evaluation is enqueued (zero broker enqueues). Same posture as stop_workload.

S-BIR-HANDLER-TXN (US-BIR-1 AC3/4): a #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/restart_workload_intent_key.rs (sibling of job_stop_intent_key.rs) given a declared workloads/payments aggregate (and an optional /stop sentinel) proves restart_workload commits exactly ONE IntentStore::txn carrying [IncrementU64{for_workload_generation(payments)}, Delete{for_workload_stop(payments)}] (captured op-set via a counting double) AND store.get(for_workload(payments)) is Some after (intent retained, distinct from #211) AND one job-lifecycle evaluation is enqueued AND it returns 200 with { workload_id: "payments", outcome }.

S-BIR-HANDLER-OUTCOME-RESUMED (DDD-11): a #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/restart_workload_outcome.rs given a declared 'payments' whose /stop sentinel IS present at the check-exists read proves the RestartWorkloadResponse.outcome == Resumed (classified from the /stop presence at the single point-in-time check-exists read, before the bump txn; the label is cosmetic — placement is the reconciler's generation gate).

S-BIR-HANDLER-OUTCOME-RESTARTED (DDD-11): a #[tokio::test] in restart_workload_outcome.rs given a declared 'coinflip' whose /stop sentinel is ABSENT at the check-exists read proves RestartWorkloadResponse.outcome == Restarted (classified from the absent /stop).

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design') — build EXACTLY the HTTP surface ADR-0073 § 'The six pinned signatures' item 2 (+ the handler sequence in item 6) names; invent no surface beyond it. Land: restart_workload in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs mirroring stop_workload, following the item-6 sequence (parse via parse_workload_id_path → ONE point-in-time read of for_workload(id) [absent ⇒ NotFound{resource: workloads/<id>}] + for_workload_stop(id) for the cosmetic label [present ⇒ Resumed, absent ⇒ Restarted, does NOT gate placement] → ONE store.txn([IncrementU64{for_workload_generation(id)}, Delete{for_workload_stop(id)}]) → enqueue_workload_lifecycle_eval → 200); RestartWorkloadResponse + #[serde(rename_all=snake_case)] RestartOutcome{Restarted,Resumed} in api.rs (sibling of StopWorkloadResponse/StopOutcome); the .route("/v1/jobs/:id/restart", post(handlers::restart_workload)) in lib.rs (mirrors the /v1/jobs/:id/stop route). HTTP status: 200 success, 404 NotFound, 400 malformed id, 500 store error. The handler NEVER reads the generation value (increment is store-side per item 4) and NEVER deletes workloads/<id> (intent stays declared).

RED convention (per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'RED scaffolds'): restart_workload + RestartWorkloadResponse/RestartOutcome + the route land RED via todo!("RED scaffold: restart_workload handler") with #[expect(clippy::todo, reason = "RED scaffold; lands GREEN in step 01-03")]; the three restart_workload_*.rs acceptance scaffolds land RED via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]; RED→GREEN→COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md (every scenario REDs MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY).

Mutation gate (the shared per-step invocation in validation.mutation_invocation; --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs): kill-rate ≥ 80%. KILL TARGETS: the 404 posture (a mutation that bumps/enqueues on an absent aggregate flips S-BIR-HANDLER-404); the one-txn [IncrementU64, Delete] op set (S-BIR-HANDLER-TXN); the present⇒Resumed / absent⇒Restarted label classification (S-BIR-HANDLER-OUTCOME-RESUMED/-RESTARTED). The IncrementU64 store-arm itself is mutation-gated at 01-01; this step's new mutable surface is the handler decision logic.
steps.scenario_name: S-BIR-HANDLER-404/-TXN/-OUTCOME-RESUMED/-RESTARTED — restart on an unknown id is an honest 404 with no mutation; a declared restart commits one atomic bump+clear txn, retains the intent row, enqueues one eval; the cosmetic outcome label is classified from the /stop check-exists read

steps.name: overdrive workload restart CLI verb — closes the e2e vertical slice
criteria: S-BIR-CLI-RESTART-SUCCESS (US-BIR-1, the e2e production loop): a #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/workload_restart.rs (NEW; gated integration-tests) is a DIRECT CLI HANDLER-CALL against an in-process run_server — NO subprocess, NO CARGO_BIN_EXE_overdrive, per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md § 'Integration tests — no subprocess' (a firm rule rejecting Command::spawn; sibling of the direct-call precedents endpoint_from_config.rs / deploy.rs / alloc_status.rs). Given a running in-process control plane (overdrive serve / run_server on an ephemeral port, trust triple written by serve) with a declared workload 'payments', it proves overdrive_cli::commands::workload::restart(RestartArgs { id: "payments", config_path }).await returns Ok(RestartOutput { workload_id: "payments", outcome }) with outcome ∈ { restarted, resumed }. The @real-io proof for the get/delete/route path: the CLI handler resolves the endpoint from the trust triple and POSTs /v1/jobs/:id/restart through the production route → real LocalIntentStore in run_server (pipeline-level handler-internal tests do NOT substitute per docs/analysis/rca-user-port-gap.md). main.rs dispatch is the thin reviewed mutants::skip dispatcher (it calls the SAME commands::workload::restart this test calls — same posture as deploy/stop/alloc); the verb's real-kernel end-to-end path is the Tier-3 oracle ATs driving the route.

S-BIR-CLI-RESTART-UNKNOWN (US-BIR-1 AC5): a #[tokio::test] in workload_restart.rs (DIRECT CLI HANDLER-CALL, NO subprocess) given a running in-process control plane with no declared workload 'nonexistent' proves overdrive_cli::commands::workload::restart(RestartArgs { id: "nonexistent", config_path }).await returns Err(CliError::HttpStatus { status: 404, error: "not_found", .. }) AND render::cli_error_to_exit_code(&err) is non-zero — the CLI maps the handler 404 to an honest typed error → a non-zero exit code, not a silent success.

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design') — the verb's six surfaces per ADR-0073 § 'The six pinned signatures' items 1/3 (build EXACTLY those signatures; invent no surface beyond them): (a) the cli.rs Command::Workload(WorkloadCommand)/WorkloadCommand::Restart{id} clap arm (new top-level workload namespace, NOT under job, #220-aligned); (b) the NEW module crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/workload.rs (sibling of deploy.rs) carrying RestartArgs/RestartOutput + pub async fn restart per ADR-0073 item 1 (RestartOutcome re-exported from overdrive_control_plane::api; the Restart handler lives on commands::workload, NOT deploy); (c) ApiClient::restart_workload per ADR-0073 item 3 (POST v1/jobs/{id}/restart via post_typed, 404 → CliError::HttpStatus body.error "not_found", sibling of stop_workload). The Job subcommand stays list/stop only.

BINARY DISPATCH SURFACE (REQUIRED — review HIGH-2; the 'mechanism built, wiring deferred' trap, CLAUDE.md § 'Build vertical slices through production entry points'): today main.rs::run() ends with a catch-all `other => { tracing::warn!("command not yet wired"); Ok(()) }`, so an UN-wired WorkloadCommand::Restart would silently exit 0 with no effect. This step OWNS the binary wiring: (a) a Command::Workload(WorkloadCommand::Restart { id }) arm in main.rs::run() that calls commands::workload::restart(RestartArgs { id, config_path: default_config_path() }), prints the new renderer on Ok, and on Err maps via render::cli_error + render::cli_error_to_exit_code (mirror the existing Command::Job(JobCommand::Stop) / Command::Alloc(AllocCommand::Status) arms); (b) pub mod workload; added to crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/mod.rs; (c) render::workload_restart_accepted(&RestartOutput) -> String in crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs (sibling of workload_stop_accepted), called by the new main.rs arm. ACCEPTANCE: the operator-facing path actually restarts + renders through main.rs — the Restart arm is removed from the unwired catch-all, not left to fall through to the warn+exit-0 path. main.rs is the thin reviewed `mutants::skip` dispatcher (same posture as deploy/stop/alloc); the end-to-end production loop is closed at the HTTP route by the Tier-3 oracle ATs (02-01/03-01/04-01, which drive POST /v1/jobs/:id/restart).

RED convention (per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'RED scaffolds'): WorkloadCommand::Restart dispatch + commands::workload::restart + ApiClient::restart_workload land RED via todo!("RED scaffold: overdrive workload restart verb") with #[expect(clippy::todo, reason = "RED scaffold; lands GREEN in step 01-04")]; the workload_restart.rs direct-handler-call scaffolds land RED via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]; RED→GREEN→COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md (both scenarios RED MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY).

Vertical-slice gate (CLAUDE.md 'Build vertical slices through production entry points'): this step closes the Phase-01 e2e loop — overdrive serve + overdrive deploy + overdrive workload restart, exercised by S-BIR-CLI-RESTART-SUCCESS as a direct CLI handler-call through an in-process run_server (NO subprocess, per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md), with the verb's real-kernel end-to-end path covered by the Tier-3 oracle ATs driving POST /v1/jobs/:id/restart. No test installs/clears an intent key, binds a socket, or supplies an address production omits.

Mutation gate (the shared per-step invocation in validation.mutation_invocation; --package overdrive-cli --file crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/workload.rs --file crates/overdrive-cli/src/http_client.rs): kill-rate ≥ 80%. KILL TARGETS: the CLI handler decision + RestartOutput shape (S-BIR-CLI-RESTART-SUCCESS); the 404→non-zero-exit mapping — a mutation that swallows the 404 / exits 0 on an unknown id MUST be killed by S-BIR-CLI-RESTART-UNKNOWN. main.rs and render::workload_restart_accepted are the thin mutants::skip dispatcher/renderer (not in this gate's mutable surface, per the deploy/stop/alloc posture).
steps.scenario_name: S-BIR-CLI-RESTART-SUCCESS/-UNKNOWN — the restart verb parses, dispatches through serve + deploy + restart, and reports the new instance; an unknown id maps to an honest non-zero not-found

phases.name: Stable frontend survives the cycle (US-BIR-2 stable-F half, slice-02)

steps.name: Stable F across the cycle — un-ignore S-DBN-WS-STABLE driving the production verb
criteria: S-DBN-WS-STABLE (US-BIR-1 + US-BIR-2, K-BIR-1/2; @oracle — already authored, un-ignore → GREEN): the EXISTING Tier-3 AT answered_frontend_is_byte_stable_across_alloc_cycle_next_connect_lands_new_backend in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs (line ~1685) is un-ignored and its stop_and_converge + same-spec-redeploy cycle is swapped for the production `overdrive workload restart server` action (POST /v1/jobs/:id/restart, per ADR-0073); the unchanged assertions then prove alloc_b1 ≠ alloc_b2 (new AllocationId) AND f1_again == f1 byte-for-byte (F retained across the cycle) AND the next connect to F1 lands the new backend B2 byte-exact AND F1 was always a stable frontend ∈ 10.98.0.0/16, never a per-instance backend addr ∈ 10.99.0.0/16 AND the inter-agent leg-B↔leg-C hop carries TLS 1.3 application_data records (0x17), zero cleartext.

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): this step introduces NO new production src — the mesh path (FrontendAddrAllocator idempotent assign, the re-keyed MtlsResolve per-connect live-backend translation) is SHIPPED (#243 / #26 / #236) and the verb is built in Phase 01. The ONLY edit is in the EXISTING AT: remove the #[ignore = "02-02 DEFERRED to overdrive-sh/overdrive#249 …"] attribute string ENTIRELY (removed, not rewritten — no stale forward-pointer) and replace the stop_and_converge(&skeleton, server) + deploy_and_wait_running(… server_service_spec …) cycle with the production overdrive workload restart server action. The assertions and the wire-capture oracle are unchanged. Invent NO surface — no second source of backend truth; the resolved F is the addr MtlsResolve.resolve recognizes and classifies Mesh.

Three-AT division (stated explicitly so DELIVER has no ambiguity): the three #249-deferred oracle ATs are split across 02-01 / 03-01 / 04-01 by the carpaccio/vertical-slice rule — each of 02-01 and 03-01 closes a real production loop by un-ignoring ITS OWN oracle AT, and 04-01 is the terminal gate. THIS step (02-01) un-ignores ONLY S-DBN-WS-STABLE (the stable-F-across-cycle half, US-BIR-2 stable-F). S-DBN-CHURN is 03-01's; S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02-RECOVERY is 04-01's; 04-01 then confirms ALL THREE green on the pinned-6.18 matrix.

RED convention (oracle, NOT a MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY scaffold per distill/red-classification.md § 'The oracle ATs are NOT MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY scaffolds'): the AT is #[ignore]'d today, so its 'RED' is the ignore skip — there is no todo!/should_panic scaffold here. The gate is un-ignore → GREEN on the pinned-6.18 Tier-3 matrix driving the production verb.

Mutation gate: SKIPPED for this step — it touches no mutable production src (the only edit is an EXISTING integration test: removing an #[ignore] attribute and swapping the cycle mechanism to the production verb). The reconciler/handler/store mutable surfaces are mutation-gated at 01-01/01-02/01-03; the FrontendAddrAllocator F-retention is already Tier-1 mutation-gated by the dial-by-name arc (S-DBN-FRONTEND-03 / S-DBN-IDX-02).

Tier-3 execution (the merge gate, ADR-0068): the AT must actually RUN green on the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix — dev-Lima is necessary-but-not-sufficient. Run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(answered_frontend_is_byte_stable_across_alloc_cycle_next_connect_lands_new_backend)' (NOT --no-run — a --no-run gate is green even when the fixture refuses to boot); sweep leaked Lima cgroup/XDP/nft/netns state before re-running (reference_pre_push_flaky_foundational_crate_lima_cleanup).
steps.scenario_name: S-DBN-WS-STABLE — the name re-resolves the same stable address across the cycle and the next connect lands the new backend (oracle un-ignored, driving the production restart verb)

phases.name: In-flight churn fails fast (US-BIR-2 churn half, slice-03)

steps.name: A1 — forward a clean backend FIN as a leg-F half-close (pump half-close-forward) + long-lived full-duplex test backend
criteria: A1 PRODUCTION FIX (ADR-0070 amendment 2026-07-01, Root Cause A): on a PumpExit::Graceful that is a genuine SOURCE clean-close (a peer FIN observed on the pump's source leg — POLLHUP-with-no-POLLIN, n_in==0, or the splice_pipe_to_dst n_out==0 dst-EOF — NOT a deliberate teardown), the pump forwards the close to its OPPOSING leg via shutdown(dst_fd, SHUT_WR) BEFORE mark_exited, then still exits Graceful (NON-reclaiming). Every pump already holds its own dst_fd as the opposing leg (OUTBOUND return decrypt: src=legB→dst=legF is THE churn fix; OUTBOUND forward encrypt: dst=legB; INBOUND deliver decrypt: dst=legS; INBOUND response encrypt: dst=legC). Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): edit ONLY crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/splice.rs — the terminal-exit path of run_decrypt_pump + run_encrypt_pump (alongside the existing mark_exited call). Invent NO new PumpExit variant unless the existing Graceful + stop-guard genuinely cannot express the source-EOF-vs-teardown distinction — if it cannot, STOP and surface the gap (do NOT improvise). NO change to mark_exited's reclaim gate (Graceful still does NOT fire self-teardown — half-close correctness preserved). NO sock_destroy (#61). NO new pump parameter (dst_fd is already held).

S-CHURN-HALFCLOSE-FORWARD (Tier-2 unit, the A1 driving port; crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/splice.rs #[cfg(test)] mod, sibling of graceful_eof_exit_does_not_fire_self_teardown): a #[test] over the pump's terminal-exit DECISION proves a SOURCE clean-close (Graceful with stop==false) forwards shutdown(SHUT_WR) to the dst leg — asserted by the observable that a socketpair peer on the dst leg's READ side surfaces EOF after the source FIN — AND mark_exited still does NOT fire self-teardown on Graceful (half-close correctness). Use a real AF_UNIX socketpair as dst (as the existing register test does); assert the dst peer read returns 0 (EOF) after the forward. This is the mutation-killing proof for A1.

S-CHURN-TEARDOWN-NO-FORWARD (Tier-2 unit): a #[test] proves a DELIBERATE teardown (stop==true, which also breaks Graceful at the top-of-loop) does NOT forward a half-close — the reclaim path owns the close; a redundant SHUT_WR here is wrong. Assert the dst leg is NOT shut down by the pump when stop was set (the reclaim_connection path closes it instead). Pins the !state.stop guard on the forward (the SAME stop-guard fire_self_teardown_if_unexpected uses).

T1 TEST-MODEL FIX (RCA Root Cause C, necessary regardless of A1): in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs, make server_service_spec (line ~746) model a LONG-LIVED, FULL-DUPLEX backend that does NOT close after one response — the per-accept handler keeps the connection open (loop reading + responding, or block after the first response) rather than `finally: c.close()` after one sendall. NOTE this is a SHARED spec used by S-DBN-WS-STABLE (02-01) too — confirm the long-lived shape does not regress the stable-F AT (it should not: that AT does a single round-trip then cycles; a server that stays open is strictly more permissive). Without T1 the churn AT hangs identically with NO churn (single-shot server already half-closed the backend leg before the churn window).

T2 TEST-MODEL FIX (RCA Root Cause C, dependent on A1): in churn_in_flight_read (line ~2063), promote the discarded first read (`let _ = tcp.read(&mut buf)` at ~2092) to an EXPLICIT `got == RESPONSE` byte-exact gate BEFORE holding the connection open (the same shape the working dial() helper uses at ~883) — so a future regression where the hold socket was never plumbed through B1 (H3-shaped) is caught rather than masked. The Given clause of S-DBN-CHURN ('a genuinely-live full-duplex connection') is only honest once T2 asserts the first round-trip completed.

Mutation gate (MANDATORY — A1 is new pump logic; the shared per-step invocation in validation.mutation_invocation; --package overdrive-dataplane --file crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/splice.rs): kill-rate ≥ 80%. KILL TARGETS: a mutation deleting the shutdown(SHUT_WR) forward MUST be killed by S-CHURN-HALFCLOSE-FORWARD (the dst peer would never see EOF); a mutation dropping the !state.stop guard (forwarding on a deliberate teardown) MUST be killed by S-CHURN-TEARDOWN-NO-FORWARD; a mutation flipping Graceful→TransportDeath (firing self-teardown, breaking half-close correctness) MUST be killed by the retained graceful_eof_exit_does_not_fire_self_teardown. RED convention: the A1 terminal-exit forward lands RED via a #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] unit scaffold that flips GREEN when the forward is implemented; the T1/T2 test edits are test-model changes, not RED scaffolds (they make an existing #[ignore]'d AT droppable at 03-02).

Tier-3 confirmation (necessary-but-not-yet-the-gate): after A1 + T1/T2 land, a LOCAL Lima run of the (still #[ignore]'d) churn AT via `#[ignore]`-override or a temporary un-ignore MUST show the in-flight connection fails fast (well under CHURN_BOUND) on a CLEAN restart — proving A1 closes the gap on the real datapath BEFORE 03-02 removes the #[ignore]. The formal un-ignore→GREEN gate is 03-02; this is the crafter's A1-works confirmation. Run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(in_flight_connection_fails_fast_on_backend_churn_subsequent_connect_lands_new_backend)' (NOT --no-run); sweep leaked Lima cgroup/XDP/nft/netns state before re-running (reference_pre_push_flaky_foundational_crate_lima_cleanup).
steps.scenario_name: S-CHURN-HALFCLOSE-FORWARD / S-CHURN-TEARDOWN-NO-FORWARD + T1/T2 — a clean directional backend FIN forwards as a leg-F half-close so the in-flight client fails fast; a deliberate teardown does not double-forward; the test backend is long-lived full-duplex and the first round-trip is proven live before the hold

steps.name: In-flight churn fail-fast — un-ignore S-DBN-CHURN driving the production verb
criteria: S-DBN-CHURN (US-BIR-2 churn boundary, K-BIR-3; @oracle — un-ignore → GREEN, now that A1 + T1/T2 landed at 03-01): the EXISTING Tier-3 AT in_flight_connection_fails_fast_on_backend_churn_subsequent_connect_lands_new_backend in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs (line ~1855) is un-ignored and its stop_and_converge + same-spec-redeploy cycle is swapped for the production `overdrive workload restart server` action (per ADR-0073) fired mid-connection; the unchanged assertions then prove a client holding a GENUINELY-LIVE in-flight connection through F1 to backend B1 gets a prompt reset/error/EOF (the A1 half-close forward for the clean SIGTERM'd close; upper-bounded by CHURN_BOUND / TCP_USER_TIMEOUT for transport death), never an indefinite hang, AND a subsequent fresh connect to F1 lands the new live backend B2 (byte-exact) AND NO sock_destroy is used (#61 scope).

Dependency on 03-01 (the mis-sizing fix): this step is NO LONGER 'test-only, no production src'. It depends on the A1 production pump half-close-forward AND the T1/T2 test-model fix, BOTH landed at 03-01. Without 03-01 the AT hangs to CHURN_BOUND on a clean restart (Root Cause A) or hangs identically with no churn (Root Cause C). This step is the pure un-ignore once 03-01 made the datapath + test model correct.

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): this step introduces NO new production src (A1 landed at 03-01). The ONLY edit is in the EXISTING AT: remove the #[ignore = "02-02 DEFERRED to overdrive-sh/overdrive#249 …"] attribute string ENTIRELY (removed, not rewritten — no stale forward-pointer) and replace the stop_and_converge + same-spec-redeploy cycle with the production overdrive workload restart server action fired mid-connection. The fail-fast / next-dial-live assertions and the bounded-elapsed measurement are unchanged. Invent NO surface — NO sock_destroy (#61 scope).

Three-AT division (stated explicitly): THIS step (03-02) un-ignores ONLY S-DBN-CHURN (the churn-boundary half, US-BIR-2 churn). S-DBN-WS-STABLE is 02-01's; S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02-RECOVERY is 04-01's; 04-01 confirms ALL THREE green on the pinned-6.18 matrix.

Mutation gate: SKIPPED for this step — it touches no mutable production src (the only edit is an EXISTING integration test: removing an #[ignore] attribute and swapping the cycle mechanism). The A1 pump half-close-forward is mutation-gated at 03-01; the intercept-worker/transparent-mtls path is already mutation-gated by its own arc.

Tier-3 execution (the merge gate, ADR-0068): the AT must actually RUN green on the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix — dev-Lima is necessary-but-not-sufficient. Run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(in_flight_connection_fails_fast_on_backend_churn_subsequent_connect_lands_new_backend)' (NOT --no-run); sweep leaked Lima cgroup/XDP/nft/netns state before re-running.
steps.scenario_name: S-DBN-CHURN — an in-flight connection fails fast on backend churn, then the next dial lands the new backend (oracle un-ignored, driving the production restart verb; gated on the A1 half-close-forward + T1/T2 test-model fix at 03-01)

phases.name: Terminal Tier-3 oracle gate — all three ATs green on the pinned-6.18 matrix (slice-04, DoD)

steps.name: Un-ignore the recovery AT and confirm all three oracle ATs green on the pinned-6.18 matrix
criteria: S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02-RECOVERY (US-BIR-1 + US-BIR-2, K-BIR-2; @oracle — already authored, un-ignore → GREEN): the EXISTING Tier-3 AT recovered_job_after_stop_resolves_to_the_same_stable_frontend in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_nxdomain.rs (line ~1068) is un-ignored and its recovery is swapped for the production `overdrive workload restart server` action (per ADR-0073); the unchanged assertions then prove that after 'server' was stopped via POST /v1/jobs/server/stop (its name resolves NXDOMAIN while stopped), recovering the SAME server to Running-AND-HEALTHY via the restart verb makes getent re-resolve the SAME stable F1 byte-for-byte (the allocator withheld, did not release, F across the stop — the withhold-not-release Tier-3 getent recovery observable).

Terminal gate — ALL THREE oracle ATs GREEN on the pinned-6.18 matrix (DoD, US-BIR-1 + US-BIR-2): with S-DBN-WS-STABLE un-ignored at 02-01 and S-DBN-CHURN un-ignored at 03-02 (gated on the A1 half-close-forward + T1/T2 test-model fix at 03-01), this step un-ignores the third (S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02-RECOVERY) and confirms all three (S-DBN-WS-STABLE, S-DBN-CHURN, S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02-RECOVERY) GREEN on the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix (the merge gate; dev-Lima is necessary-but-not-sufficient — ADR-0068). This is the feature's terminal quality gate / Definition of Done.

Three-AT division (stated explicitly so there is no ambiguity at DELIVER): the three #249-deferred oracle ATs are divided across 02-01 / 03-02 / 04-01 — 02-01 un-ignores S-DBN-WS-STABLE, 03-02 un-ignores S-DBN-CHURN (after 03-01 lands the A1 pump half-close-forward + T1/T2 test-model fix), and THIS step (04-01) un-ignores S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02-RECOVERY (the third/last) AND is the terminal gate that confirms ALL THREE green together on the pinned-6.18 matrix. By the carpaccio/vertical-slice rule 02-01 and 03-02 each close their own production loop by un-ignoring their own AT when they land; 04-01 lands the recovery AT and the whole-feature DoD.

No-stale-forward-pointer (CLAUDE.md 'Deferrals require GitHub issues' / .claude/rules/testing.md § '#[ignore]'): ALL THREE #[ignore = "…#249…"] attribute strings are REMOVED entirely (removed, not rewritten — no stale #249 forward-pointer remains in any of the three ATs). The strings to remove are the '02-02 DEFERRED to overdrive-sh/overdrive#249 …' attributes on the two walking-skeleton ATs and the '03-01 DEFERRED to overdrive-sh/overdrive#249 …' attribute on the nxdomain recovery AT.

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): NO new production src — the F-retention/withhold-not-release path is SHIPPED (its Tier-1 invariant is already mutation-gated by the dial-by-name arc at S-DBN-FRONTEND-03 / S-DBN-IDX-02; only the Tier-3 getent recovery observable was #249-blocked) and the verb is built in Phase 01. The ONLY edit is in the EXISTING AT: remove the #[ignore] string entirely and swap the recovery mechanism for the production overdrive workload restart server action. Invent NO surface — no AT installs/clears a rule/key, binds a socket, or supplies an address production does not itself install/clear/bind/supply.

RED convention (oracle, NOT a MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY scaffold): the recovery AT is #[ignore]'d today, so its 'RED' is the ignore skip. The gate is un-ignore → GREEN on the pinned-6.18 Tier-3 matrix driving the production verb.

Mutation gate: SKIPPED for this step — it touches no mutable production src (the only edits are EXISTING integration tests: removing the three #[ignore] attributes and swapping the recovery mechanism to the production verb). The feature's mutable production surfaces are mutation-gated at 01-01 (store primitive) and 01-02 (reconciler logic) and 01-03 (handler).

Tier-3 execution (the MERGE GATE, ADR-0068): all three ATs must actually RUN green on the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix — dev-Lima is necessary-but-not-sufficient, and a --no-run gate is green even when the fixture refuses to boot. Run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(answered_frontend_is_byte_stable_across_alloc_cycle_next_connect_lands_new_backend) + test(in_flight_connection_fails_fast_on_backend_churn_subsequent_connect_lands_new_backend) + test(recovered_job_after_stop_resolves_to_the_same_stable_frontend)' (NOT --no-run); sweep leaked Lima cgroup/XDP/nft/netns state before re-running (reference_pre_push_flaky_foundational_crate_lima_cleanup).
steps.scenario_name: S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02-RECOVERY — a recovered workload re-resolves the same stable frontend (withhold-not-release); terminal gate confirms all three oracle ATs green on the pinned-6.18 matrix

mutation_invocation.note: Shared per-step mutation invocation (DRY — referenced by every step's mutation gate so the full command-line is not re-transcribed per step, addressing review BLOCKER-1's legitimate second-SSOT kernel). Each mutation-gated step runs, per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Mutation testing': cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package <step package> --file <step file(s)>. The per-step --package/--file scoping and the specific KILL TARGETS are named in each step's mutation-gate criterion; the kill-rate gate is ≥ 80% (per-PR diff-scoped) for every gated step. macOS routes through `cargo xtask lima run --` because --features integration-tests participates (memory: Lima mutation summary lands in the GUEST target dir — read the guest summary / run log, not the host target/xtask/mutants-summary.json). Steps 02-01/03-02/04-01 are mutation-SKIPPED (they touch no mutable production src — only an #[ignore] removal + cycle-mechanism swap on an EXISTING integration test); their feature surfaces are mutation-gated at 01-01/01-02/01-03 and (for the A1 pump half-close-forward) at 03-01, plus the dial-by-name arc for the FrontendAddrAllocator / intercept-worker. Step 03-01 IS mutation-gated (--package overdrive-dataplane --file crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/splice.rs) — the A1 half-close-forward is new pump logic (ADR-0070 amendment 2026-07-01).

concision_waiver.gate: codex_nw_review concision gate (BLOCKER-2 / concision_and_precision FAILED): 1,500-word budget for a 4-8 step roadmap, ≤ 5 criteria per step, ≤ ~30 words per criterion.
concision_waiver.authority: CLAUDE.md § 'Roadmap validator warnings' (Overdrive roadmap ACs DELIBERATELY carry scenario-level specificity — test names, invariant names, proptest targets, kill-rate thresholds — and tightening them to defaults loses traceability; the validator exits 0 anyway) + CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design — never invent API surface' (the anti-drift discipline these ACs exist to enforce).
concision_waiver.precedent: The 65+ in-tree roadmaps under docs/feature/**/deliver/roadmap.json pin ADR signatures + scenario-level specificity; the direct sibling docs/feature/dial-by-name-responder/deliver/roadmap.json (43 S-DBN-* scenarios across 10 steps, each step pinning ADR-0072 signatures + per-step mutation command-lines + kill targets) is the canonical shape this roadmap follows.
concision_waiver.scope: The waiver covers the word-count budget, the ≤5-criteria-per-step cap, and the ≤30-words-per-criterion cap. It does NOT waive the review's two legitimate non-concision findings: (a) the second-SSOT / implementation-recipe kernel (BLOCKER-1 / ac_implementation_coupling / implementation_code_in_roadmap) is addressed by the DRY pass — verbatim ADR-0073 Rust signatures, serde attributes, and redb match-arm algorithms were replaced with REFERENCES to ADR-0073 § 'The six pinned signatures', and the repeated full mutation command-lines were collapsed into validation.mutation_invocation above, while each step KEEPS its scenario IDs, invariant names, proptest targets, and specific KILL TARGETS; (b) the CLI binary-dispatch surface (HIGH-2) is added to step 01-04; (c) the private-helper test boundary (HIGH-3) is addressed by S-BIR-REGRESSION-NUMERIC proving the numeric-vs-lexical current-instance invariant through reconcile() at the driving port, alongside the sanctioned pure-fn proptest S-BIR-CURRENT-ALLOC.
concision_waiver.des_roadmap_validate: des-roadmap validate exits 0 — the STEP_NAME_TOO_LONG / CRITERIA_TOO_LONG / DESCRIPTION_TOO_LONG warnings are cosmetic and non-blocking per CLAUDE.md § 'Roadmap validator warnings'.
